type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Using the emission factors on Tables 16 and 17 on Page 5-18 of CAPP's "A Recommended Approach to Completing the NPRI for the Upstream Oil and Gas Industry" March 2007, we calculated 3.414 tonnes NOx from our boilers and 1.937 tonnes NOx from our East emergence flare stack. type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
Reciprocating compressors ran more hours and low NOx gas turbine unit ran less hours although there was decreased fuel consumption.
There were also increased compressor venting and fugitive emissions from 2024 survey data.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Previous estimation method utilized emission factors from Table 16: Input Fuel-Based Equipment Emission Factors for Level 1 Combustion CAC Emission Calculations found in the document "March 2007 A Recommended Approach to Completing the NPRI for the Upstream Oil and Gas Industry". However, in 2022, Aitken used Particulate Matter emission factors from Gas Turbines illustrated in AP-42 volume 1-3.1 Stationary Gas Turbines table 3.1.2a.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
- Increase in stack and point releases due to increase in production.
- The estimation of (VOC) emissions from fugitive releases in 2021 was performed using an emission factor derived from Table 17: Process Emissions for Level 1 CAC Emission Calculations found in the document "March 2007 A Recommended Approach to Completing the NPRI for the Upstream Oil and Gas Industry". However, in 2022, Aitken conducted fugitive measurements through Leak Detection and Repair (LDAR) methods, and the VOC emissions were recalculated based on the detected volumes. As a result, there was a significant decrease in the reported VOC emissions.
